---
name: deep-scan
description: "Comprehensive project analysis. Generates topic-based docs in .tstack/docs/ and a compressed pipe-delimited Project Index for copilot-instructions.md. Invoked by Scout or Orchestrator."
user-invocable: false
---

# Deep Scan — Comprehensive Project Analysis

Perform a thorough analysis of the project codebase and generate both full documentation and a compressed index for passive context.

## Pre-requisites

1. Verify `.tstack/project.md` exists (surface scan must be done first). If missing, run the Scout surface scan before proceeding.
2. Read `.tstack/project.md` to understand the tech stack, languages, and frameworks.

## Analysis Categories

Scan the codebase for ALL of the following. Skip categories that don't apply to the project (e.g., no API surface for a pure library).

### Category 1: Module/Component Map
- Enumerate top-level directories and their purpose
- Identify entry points (main files, index files, route registrations)
- Trace import/export relationships to build an internal module dependency graph
- Identify module boundaries (what depends on what)

### Category 2: API Surface
- Route definitions (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, CLI commands)
- Exported functions and classes from public modules
- Event emitters and listeners
- Message queue producers/consumers

### Category 3: Data Flows
- Data entry points (user input, API calls, file reads, queue consumption)
- Transformation and validation pipeline
- Persistence targets (database writes, file writes, cache stores)
- State management patterns (stores, context, sessions, caches)

### Category 4: Error Handling Patterns
- Error types (custom error classes, error codes, Result types)
- Try/catch conventions and error boundary locations
- Logging patterns (logger library, log levels, structured logging)
- User-facing error formatting

### Category 5: Configuration & Environment
- Environment variables referenced (names, where used, documented defaults)
- Config files and schemas (dotenv, YAML config, JSON config)
- Feature flags and toggles
- Deployment profiles (dev, staging, production)

### Category 6: Test Coverage Map
- Test directory structure and framework
- Which source modules have corresponding test files
- Which source modules have NO tests (coverage gaps)
- Test patterns used (unit, integration, e2e, snapshot)
- Fixture and mock patterns

### Category 7: Security Boundaries
- Authentication mechanism and middleware location
- Authorization / role-based access control points
- Input validation layers (schemas, validators, sanitizers)
- Secret management approach (env vars, vaults, config)
- CORS, CSP, and security header configuration

### Category 8: Build Pipeline
- Build commands and tool chain
- CI/CD configuration files and workflow
- Deployment manifests (Docker, K8s, serverless)
- Asset processing (bundling, compilation, transpilation)

### Category 9: Cross-Cutting Concerns
- Logging injection points
- Caching layers and invalidation patterns
- Internationalization (i18n) setup
- Middleware chains and interception points
- Telemetry and monitoring hooks

### Category 10: Dependencies
- External dependencies with version constraints
- Internal module dependency graph (which internal modules depend on which)
- Deprecated or outdated dependencies (check for major version gaps)
- Dependency injection patterns

## Output Phase 1: Full Documentation

Create `.tstack/docs/` directory (if it doesn't exist) and write these topic files. Each file should be comprehensive, human-readable Markdown.

| File | Content Source |
|:---|:---|
| `architecture.md` | Categories 1, 3 — component map, data flow diagrams (text), module boundaries |
| `api-surface.md` | Category 2 — all public APIs, route maps, exported interfaces |
| `conventions.md` | Categories 4, cross-cutting patterns — naming, file structure, error handling, patterns discovered |
| `testing.md` | Category 6 — test framework, coverage map, testing patterns, gaps |
| `security.md` | Category 7 — auth model, input validation, secret management |
| `build-and-deploy.md` | Category 8 — build commands, CI/CD pipeline, deployment targets |
| `dependencies.md` | Category 10 — external deps table, internal module graph |

Each file should have a header noting it was generated by deep-scan and should not be hand-edited:
```
<!-- Generated by deep-scan skill. Re-run deep-scan to update. -->
```

## Output Phase 2: Compressed Index

Generate a pipe-delimited compressed index from all analysis categories. This index goes into `.github/copilot-instructions.md` between the `<!-- #region T-STACK:PROJECT-INDEX -->` markers.

### Index Format Specification

```
## Project Index
<!-- deep-scan | [ISO-8601 timestamp] | [commit hash or "uncommitted"] -->

STACK|[language(s)]|[framework(s)]|[build tool]|[test framework]|[linter]
ENTRY|[main entry point path(s)]
BUILD|[build cmd]|TEST|[test cmd]|LINT|[lint cmd]|FMT|[fmt cmd]

MOD|path|type|depends-on|exports-summary
[one line per significant module/directory]

API|method|path|handler-location|auth|validation
[one line per route/endpoint, skip if no API]

TEST|module-path|test-file|framework|coverage-level
[one line per tested module, note untested modules as NONE]

SEC|concern|location|mechanism|notes
[one line per security boundary]

CONF|name|source|used-by|default
[one line per env var or significant config value]

DEPS|name|version|purpose|status
[one line per significant external dependency]

CONV|area|pattern|example
[one line per discovered convention]
```

### Compression Rules
- Use abbreviations: `auth`=authentication, `val`=validation, `cfg`=configuration, `dep`=dependency, `fn`=function, `comp`=component, `svc`=service, `mw`=middleware, `ctrl`=controller
- Omit empty/NA fields with `-`
- Target ~8KB for the index. For large projects, prioritize: STACK > MOD > API > SEC > TEST > CONV > DEPS > CONF
- If the project is small enough that all categories fit under 8KB, include everything
- Each line should be independently parseable — no multi-line entries

### Writing the Index

Replace ONLY the content between `<!-- #region T-STACK:PROJECT-INDEX -->` and `<!-- #endregion T-STACK:PROJECT-INDEX -->` in `.github/copilot-instructions.md`. Preserve the region markers themselves and all content outside them.

## Output Phase 3: Scan Metadata

Write `.tstack/docs/.scan-metadata` (overwrite if exists):

```
last-scan: [ISO-8601 timestamp]
commit: [current HEAD short hash, or "uncommitted" if working tree is dirty]
version: [value from .tstack/.version]
categories: [comma-separated list of categories that were scanned]
index-size: [byte count of the compressed index section]
docs-files: [comma-separated list of files written to .tstack/docs/]
```

## Staleness Detection

Other agents can check scan freshness by reading `.tstack/docs/.scan-metadata`:
- If `commit` differs from current HEAD by >20 commits → recommend re-scan
- If `last-scan` is >7 days old → recommend re-scan
- If `.tstack/project.md` modified more recently than `last-scan` → recommend re-scan

## Constraints

- This skill is READ-ONLY on source code. Never modify application source files.
- `.tstack/docs/` files are state — never synced to `src/`, never overwritten by framework updates.
- The skill file itself IS framework-shipped and synced to `src/`.
- For very large codebases (500+ files), use sampling for conventions/patterns but be exhaustive for API surface and module graph.
- Flag dynamically generated code (codegen, metaprogramming) with a note rather than attempting to analyze it.
- Never copy raw string content from source files into index entries. All index values must be agent-synthesized summaries derived from structural analysis. Validate each index line matches the expected `KEYWORD|field|field|...` pipe-delimited format before writing.
